channel.title: Double rendering of items held in state array?
channel.description:
This issue may relate to this ongoing question I have here: https://community.frontity.org/t/how-to-properly-abstract-alias-custom-theme-settings/1231/9
I have a component that is a series of links held in the footer of the website. These links are determined by an addition to the theme object in src/index.js, with the intentiont hat these links could be overwritten by the frontity.settings.js file on a per install basis and as needed.
the component maps through the array and then renders a link with text content of the name of the website being linked. See [component here](https://github.com/jcklpe/desert-jackalope/blob/master/src/components/Footer/footer-links.js).
For some reason though, react is rendering the footerlinks each twice: ![image|455x500](upload://pLrOWkER9Cp3v8VDevVlMmUYg0M.png)
I'm not sure why. I ran this by a more experienced React dev friend of mine and he said he didn't understand it and that he thinks it's a framework issue or that I have misunderstood and not implemented something according to the way expected by the framework. Any clues?

item.title: Double rendering of items held in state array?
item.dc:creator: David
item.description:
Hey, we did the release, check it out!

Frontity release: New features and bug fixes 📢 Announcements
During the last month, we’ve been working on plenty of new features and bug fixes. Here you have what was included in the last release: New features TwentyTwenty theme improvements Add pagination to the theme - Feature topic. Some CSS improvements. Add proper status codes. New processor to lazy load iframes: Feature Topic. Iframe processor docs & iframe component docs. New processor to execute scripts included in the content: Feature topic. Script processor docs & script component d…

PS: remember to follow this guide of our documentation to keep Frontity and its packages updated.

Read full topic
item.source: Double rendering of items held in state array?

item.title: Double rendering of items held in state array?
item.dc:creator: thedonquixotic
item.source: Double rendering of items held in state array?

item.title: Double rendering of items held in state array?
item.dc:creator: David
item.description:
Oh, that’s probably this bug:

github.com/frontity/frontity

Array elements from package state are duplicated on `csr`

opened 04:04PM - 26 Nov 19 UTC
closed 01:22PM - 04 Feb 20 UTC
orballo

Bug report I've searched for existing issues I've checked documentation: https://docs.frontity.org I've checked community forum: https://community.frontity.org Describe the expected behavior When I define...

bug priority: high

The bug fix is merged and will be released in the next version of @frontity/core.

We’ll let you know when it’s published (very soon).

Read full topic
item.source: Double rendering of items held in state array?

item.title: Double rendering of items held in state array?
item.dc:creator: thedonquixotic
item.description:
This issue may relate to this ongoing question I have here: How to properly abstract/alias custom theme settings?

I have a component that is a series of links held in the footer of the website. These links are determined by an addition to the theme object in src/index.js, with the intentiont hat these links could be overwritten by the frontity.settings.js file on a per install basis and as needed.

the component maps through the array and then renders a link with text content of the name of the website being linked. See component here.

For some reason though, react is rendering the footerlinks each twice:

I’m not sure why. I ran this by a more experienced React dev friend of mine and he said he didn’t understand it and that he thinks it’s a framework issue or that I have misunderstood and not implemented something according to the way expected by the framework. Any clues?

Read full topic
item.source: Double rendering of items held in state array?
